title: Anthropic Listed Fable 5 Tokenizer, Fallback Billing, And Retention Constraints
summary: Anthropic's June 9 Claude Platform release notes say Claude Fable 5 uses the Opus 4.7 tokenizer family, adds refusal and fallback handling semantics, and requires 30-day data retention on the Claude API.

evidence_refs.license_note: Official release notes reviewed for factual metadata; no provider prose copied.

estimated_cost_effect.magnitude: token counts can increase versus models before Claude Opus 4.7; fallback requests are billed at the fallback model rates
estimated_cost_effect.basis: official release-note statements about tokenizer and fallback billing

impacts.recommended_action: Re-run token counting and cost forecasts before routing Fable 5 traffic, especially for long-context workloads and fallback chains.
Keep zero-retention workloads off Fable 5 until the provider documents support for those accounts or an approved exception exists.

detail.old_accounting: Pre-Fable Anthropic routes may have used older tokenizer assumptions, no Fable-specific refusal category, and existing retention eligibility rules.
detail.new_accounting: Claude Fable 5 uses the Opus 4.7 tokenizer family, no-output refusals are not billed, fallback retries are billed at fallback-model rates, and the model is not available under zero data retention.
examples: Run token counting with model claude-fable-5 before changing cost budgets.
Test fallback chains because a refused request can rerun on another model with different billing.

limitations: APW records provider-stated accounting semantics, not measured invoice deltas.
No raw release-note body is copied into the event.
